description: Bamboo Data Center REST API (/rest/api/latest). Read paths are JSON; write paths (queue/trigger, stage trigger) use form-encoded bamboo.variable.* bodies and require the X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header, and rerun uses the /build/admin/restartBuild.action Struts endpoint. Build logs are served as plain text from /download. Probed against DC 10.2.14.

endpoints.summary: Return server version, build number, state, and edition.
endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/info.json; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md endpoint GET /rest/api/latest/info → OK

verdict.reasoning: Useful for connection-test and compatibility guard. Not currently called by BambooApiClient. Could replace or supplement currentUser as the auth-test endpoint since it also requires a valid PAT.

gotchas: Returns 200 even on trial/evaluation Bamboo instances; the `edition` field is an empty string on Data Center.
`state` can be RUNNING, PAUSED, or STOPPED; PAUSED means scheduled maintenance, builds are queued but not dispatched.

endpoints.summary: Return the authenticated user's name, full name, and email. Used as an auth-test probe.
endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/current_user.json; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md endpoint GET /rest/api/latest/currentUser → OK; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Bamboo Server REST API 'GET /rest/api/latest/currentUser — test connection'

verdict.reasoning: The connection-test pattern (adopted in Jira, Bitbucket, Sonar) should be applied to Bamboo settings too. This is the natural auth-test endpoint.

gotchas: Returns 401 for an invalid/expired PAT — suitable as a lightweight auth test.
Does not verify plan-level permissions; a valid PAT with restricted scope may still 401 on plan endpoints.

endpoints.summary: Paginated list of all plans (projects × plans). Each item includes projectKey, key, shortName, buildName, shortKey, type, enabled, isActive, isBuilding, averageBuildTimeInSeconds, and sizes for stages/branches.

params.name: expand
params.description: Expand nested objects. Client passes `plans.plan` to populate the plan array.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Page size. Client uses 100.

params.name: start-index
params.description: Zero-based pagination cursor. Client computes offset as previous start-index + actual items returned — NOT the server-echoed start-index (which is unreliable).

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/plan_list.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 39 `getPlans()` → paginate loop with expand=plans.plan&max-results=100&start-index={start}; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/plan → OK
gotchas: The server-echoed `start-index` in the response is unreliable (Bamboo's `/plan/{key}/branch` echoes 0 on every page). The paginate() loop in BambooApiClient advances the cursor by requested start-index + actual items returned.
2292 plans in the probe org — without pagination this would hit Bamboo's max-results cap and silently truncate.
`type` can be `chain` (standard multi-stage), `branch`, or `maven` (legacy).

endpoints.summary: List all projects with basic metadata (key, name, planCount).

params.name: max-results
params.description: Page size. Client uses 100.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 48 `getProjects()` → GET /rest/api/latest/project?max-results=100; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture

endpoints.summary: Get a single project and expand its plan list.

params.name: projectKey
params.description: Bamboo project key (e.g. PROJ).

params.name: expand
params.description: Client passes `plans.plan` to inline the plan list.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 54 `getProjectPlans()` → GET /rest/api/latest/project/{key}?expand=plans.plan

endpoints.summary: Full-text / fuzzy search for plans by name or key fragment. Returns up to max-results matching plan stubs.

params.name: searchTerm
params.description: Search term (plan name or key fragment).

params.name: fuzzy
params.description: Enable fuzzy matching. Client passes true.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Result cap. Client uses 25.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 60 `searchPlans()` → GET /rest/api/latest/search/plans?searchTerm={q}&fuzzy=true&max-results=25
gotchas: The /search/plans path is not part of the core REST API documented on developer.atlassian.com — it's a Bamboo-specific endpoint only available on Data Center.
Fuzzy matching is case-insensitive but can return false positives for very short search terms.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve the plan's YAML spec (Bamboo Specs / infrastructure-as-code definition).

params.name: planKey
params.description: Bamboo plan key (e.g. PROJ-PLAN).

params.name: format
params.description: Response format. Client passes YAML.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 78 `getPlanSpecs()` → GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{key}/specs?format=YAML; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{planKey}/specs → FAIL (403)

verdict.reasoning: Returns 403 with a non-admin PAT on both probe bundles. The client calls it but the feature is gated by Bamboo admin permissions. Should be surfaced to the user as a graceful 403 rather than a silent error.

gotchas: Returns HTTP 403 for non-admin PATs — confirmed on both probe bundles against DC 10.2.14.
Only plans managed as Bamboo Specs (YAML-in-VCS) have a meaningful response; legacy manually-configured plans return an empty or minimal document.

endpoints.summary: List plan branches for a given master plan. Used both to enumerate branches (getBranches) and to populate the Automation branch selector (getPlanBranches). Each item has key, shortName, enabled.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Master plan key.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Page size. Client uses configurable value.

params.name: start-index
params.description: Pagination cursor. CRITICAL: server always echoes start-index: 0 for this endpoint — client must advance cursor by actual items returned.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 83 `getBranches()` and line 505 `getPlanBranches()` → GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{key}/branch?max-results={n}&start-index={start}; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture 'Bamboo's /plan/{key}/branch endpoint echoes start-index: 0 on every page' (fixed 2026-05-26, regression test BambooApiClientPaginationTest)
gotchas: CRITICAL: This endpoint echoes `start-index: 0` on EVERY page response — the paginate() loop MUST advance the cursor by `requestedStart + actualItemCount`, NOT by the server-echoed value. Trusting the server value stalls the cursor and re-fetches the same window up to the page cap, duplicating branches (bug fixed 2026-05-26).
BambooServiceImpl.getPlanBranches also applies `distinctBy { it.key }` as a defensive second deduplication layer.
Branch plan keys follow the pattern `{MASTER-KEY}-{SHORTKEY}` — the segment after the last hyphen is the branch short key.

endpoints.summary: Get plan metadata. Used both as a full getPlanInfo call and as a lightweight validatePlan check (returns boolean based on 200 vs 404).

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan key (e.g. PROJ-PLAN).

params.name: expand
params.description: Optional expand. For variableContext see the dedicated endpoint.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 166 `getPlanInfo()` and line 536 `validatePlan()` → GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{candidate}; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{planKey} → OK
gotchas: validatePlan() calls this and treats 404 as false/not-found, any 200 as valid — it does NOT parse the body.
On branch plans, the response includes a `master` block linking back to the master plan key.

endpoints.summary: Retrieve plan-level variables via variableContext expand. Returns variable[]{key, value, variableType, isPassword}. Note: uses `key` field, NOT `name`.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be `variableContext` to retrieve plan variables.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/plan_variables_via_context.json (variable[]{key,value,variableType,isPassword}); BambooApiClient.kt line 152 `getPlanVariableContext()` → GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{key}?expand=variableContext; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §variableContext finding: field is `key` not `name`

verdict.reasoning: The audit identified a DTO mismatch: the plan-level variableContext response uses `key` but the plugin's DTO originally mapped `name`. Verify the DTO field name matches `key`.

gotchas: CRITICAL: Plan-level variableContext uses `key` field — NOT `name`. Build-level variables (`?expand=variables`) use `name`. These are different response shapes for the same concept.
variableType ∈ {PLAN, GLOBAL, PROJECT}.
Password variables omit the `value` field entirely — the key is present but value is absent.
The legacy `/rest/api/latest/plan/{key}/variable` path always returns 404 on DC 10.2.14 — do not use it as a fallback (see KNOWN_UNUSED entry).

endpoints.summary: Get the latest build result for a plan key or branch plan key. Includes all stages and nested job results when expanded. Also has a branch variant: /result/{planKey}/branch/{branchName}/latest.

params.name: planOrJobKey
params.description: Plan key (PROJ-PLAN), branch plan key (PROJ-PLAN-BRANCHKEY), or chain key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Client passes `stages.stage.results.result` to inline stage and job data.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_latest_plan.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 90 `getLatestResult()` → two URL variants: /result/{key}/latest and /result/{plan}/branch/{branch}/latest, both with ?expand=stages.stage.results.result; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture 'BambooServiceImpl returns BuildResultData.stages[].jobs[] populated from ?expand=stages.stage.results.result responses'
gotchas: The branch variant `/result/{planKey}/branch/{branchName}/latest` returns 404 when branchName equals the plan's tracked branch (usually `master` or `main`) — use the plan-key variant directly for the tracked branch. Confirmed in 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md.
Each job's `buildResultKey` (e.g. `PROJ-PLAN138-UNIT-4`) is what the agent passes to `bamboo_builds.get_build_log` for per-job logs.
The `stages.stage.results.result` expand produces nested arrays; a plan-level key returns plan results, a job-level key returns job details.

endpoints.summary: Get a specific build result by full result key. Used for getBuildResult (with stages expand) and getResultVcsRevision (with vcsRevisions expand).

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Full build result key (e.g. PROJ-PLAN138 or PROJ-PLAN138-UNIT-4).

params.name: expand
params.description: Pass `stages.stage.results.result` for full stage/job data; `vcsRevisions` for commit SHA.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_full.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 467 `getBuildResult()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=stages.stage.results.result; line 125 `getResultVcsRevision()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=vcsRevisions; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Bamboo→Bitbucket bridge 'resolves the commit SHA via BambooApiClient.getResultVcsRevision'
gotchas: vcsRevisions is null/absent when the plan has no source repository linked — getResultVcsRevision returns null in that case.
The same path template serves getBuildResult (expand=stages) and getResultVcsRevision (expand=vcsRevisions) — they are separate client methods at different line numbers.

endpoints.summary: Get running and recently-queued builds for a plan. Client uses includeAllStates=true plus a small max-results to show active builds in the dashboard.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan or chain key.

params.name: includeAllStates
params.description: Include queued and building entries in addition to finished results.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Client uses 5 for the running-builds check.

params.name: expand
params.description: Client passes `stages.stage.results.result` for active build stage detail.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 273 `getRunningAndQueuedBuilds()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?includeAllStates=true&max-results=5&expand=stages.stage.results.result
gotchas: This path is overloaded with multiple client usages via different query parameters — getRunningAndQueuedBuilds and getRecentResults both call /result/{key} with different expand strings.
Without includeAllStates=true, only FINISHED results are returned; BUILDING/QUEUED builds are invisible.

endpoints.summary: Get recent build history with stage and variable detail. Client requests a configurable number of results with full stage + variable expansion for the build dashboard history list.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan or chain key.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Number of recent results. Client uses a configurable value.

params.name: expand
params.description: Client passes `results.result.stages.stage.results.result,results.result.variables.variable` for full history.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 291 `getRecentResults()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?max-results={n}&expand=results.result.stages.stage.results.result,results.result.variables.variable
gotchas: The deeply nested expand `results.result.stages.stage.results.result,results.result.variables.variable` can produce large payloads on plans with many jobs and variables.
Variables in this path use `name` (not `key`) — this is the build-level variable shape.

endpoints.summary: Find all builds associated with a specific VCS commit SHA. Used by the Bamboo→Bitbucket bridge to link failed builds to PRs.

params.name: commitSha
params.description: Full commit SHA from VCS.

params.name: expand
params.description: Client passes `results.result.plan` to get plan metadata alongside results.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_by_changeset.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 476 `getResultsByChangeset()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/byChangeset/{sha}?expand=results.result.plan; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Bamboo→Bitbucket bridge
gotchas: The `chain_branch` entries in the response also include a `master.{key}` link back to the master plan.
Only returns results where Bamboo has recorded the VCS revision; builds from plans with no linked repository are absent.

endpoints.summary: Get commit/change history for a specific build result. Returns changes.change[] with author, comment, file list.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be `changes.change` to retrieve commit history.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 522 `getBuildChanges()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=changes.change; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-1 ADOPTED
gotchas: Added as R-ADD-1 from the 2026-05-07 audit recommendations — this endpoint was not in the original client but was adopted.
Returns an empty changes array when the plan has no linked VCS repository.

endpoints.summary: Get variables attached to a specific build execution. Returns variable[]{name, value}. Note: uses `name` field at the build level (vs `key` at plan level).

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be `variables` to retrieve build-level variables.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_variables.json (variables.variable[]{name,value} with DockerTagsAsJSON payload); BambooApiClient.kt line 283 `getBuildVariables()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=variables; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture 'Build variables include dockerTagsAsJson — JSON payload of service-to-docker-tag mappings'
gotchas: CRITICAL: Build-level variables use `name` field. Plan-level variableContext uses `key` field. These are different shapes — using the wrong DTO field name is a silent mapping failure.
DockerTagsAsJSON is a JSON string value — it must be parsed a second time to extract the service→tag map.
Password-type variables: the API may omit the `value` field; callers should handle absent values gracefully.

endpoints.summary: Legacy plan-variable endpoint. Returns 404 on Bamboo DC 10.2.14 — this path was removed or never existed on DC. Do not use.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan key.

endpoints.provenance: 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §dead-fallback finding: /plan/{key}/variable always 404 on DC 10.2.14; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/plan/{planKey}/variable → FAIL (404)

verdict.reasoning: Consistently 404 on both probe bundles against DC 10.2.14. The audit recommended removing any client fallback to this path. Use /plan/{key}?expand=variableContext instead.

gotchas: This path 404s on every probe attempt — it is dead on DC 10.2.14.
If legacy code has a fallback to this endpoint, remove it; it will never return data.

endpoints.summary: Download the plain-text build log for a specific job result. Returns text/plain content, NOT JSON. Job-level result keys yield real logs (~30KB typical); plan-level keys yield a small wrapper.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Job-level build result key (appears TWICE in the path — once as directory segment, once as filename). E.g. PROJ-PLAN138-UNIT-4.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/build_log_download.json (payload_kind='text', Content-Type='text/plain;charset=utf-8', 22791 bytes); BambooApiClient.kt line 131 `getBuildLog()` → GET /download/{resultKey}/build_logs/{resultKey}.log via getRaw(); bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture 'Each BuildJobData.resultKey ... is what the agent passes to bamboo_builds.get_build_log for per-job logs'
gotchas: CRITICAL: Response is plain text, NOT JSON. The getRaw() client method must be used — do not attempt JSON deserialization.
The resultKey appears TWICE in the URL — once as the directory segment and once as the `.log` filename.
Use the JOB-level result key (e.g. PROJ-PLAN138-UNIT-4), NOT the plan-level key (PROJ-PLAN138). The plan-level key yields a tiny wrapper document, not the real job log.
This endpoint lives under /download/, not /rest/api/latest/ — it does not share the same base path as other endpoints.
Probe recorded 22791 bytes for the automation bundle log — typical job logs are ~30KB.

endpoints.summary: Get the list of artifacts produced by a build result. Returns artifacts.artifact[]{name, link.href, producerJobKey, shared}.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be `artifacts.artifact` to retrieve the artifact list.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_artifacts.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 431 `getArtifacts()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=artifacts.artifact
gotchas: The `link.href` is an absolute URL pointing to the artifact download location — pass it directly to downloadArtifact().
`shared: true` means the artifact is available to downstream jobs/plans; `shared: false` is job-private.

endpoints.summary: Download an artifact by its absolute URL from the artifact list. The URL comes from artifacts.artifact[].link.href and is not a fixed template.

params.name: artifactUrl
params.description: Absolute artifact URL from getArtifacts() response.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 438 `downloadArtifact()` → GET {artifactUrl} (arbitrary URL, not a fixed template)
gotchas: This is not a fixed REST path — the URL is opaque and comes from the artifact list response.
Artifact URLs may require the same Bearer PAT for auth, or may be publicly accessible depending on Bamboo's artifact-sharing settings.
Large artifacts (e.g. Docker image manifests, test reports) can be hundreds of MB — handle streaming appropriately.

endpoints.summary: Get test results for a build. Returns testResults.{successfulTests.testResult[], failedTests.testResult[]} each with className, methodName, status, duration.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key (plan or job level).

params.name: expand
params.description: Must include `testResults.failedTests.testResult,testResults.successfulTests.testResult`.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/result_test_results.json; BambooApiClient.kt line 141 `getTestResults()` → GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=testResults.failedTests.testResult,testResults.successfulTests.testResult
gotchas: Failed test entries include an `errors.error[].message` array with failure details — this is the primary input for BuildLogParser CVE/failure extraction.
The expand string must list both `failedTests.testResult` AND `successfulTests.testResult` explicitly; shorthand like `testResults` alone does not inline the individual test objects.
For plans with many tests, this response can be large — consider requesting only failedTests for failure-analysis use cases.

endpoints.summary: Trigger (queue) a build for a plan. Optionally selects a specific manual stage. Build variables are passed as `bamboo.variable.<key>=<value>` QUERY parameters, NOT as a JSON body.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan or chain key to trigger.

params.name: executeAllStages
params.description: Pass true to run all stages; false to run up to a specific stage.

params.name: stage
params.description: Name of the stage to execute up to (when executeAllStages=false).

params.name: bamboo.variable.*
params.description: Build variables passed as `bamboo.variable.<key>=<value>` query parameters. E.g. bamboo.variable.dockerTagsAsJson={...}. JSON body is silently ignored — variables MUST be query params.

params.name: X-Atlassian-Token
params.description: CSRF bypass header required on ALL Bamboo write operations. Value must be `no-check`.

endpoints.requestBody: Form-encoded request body via postForm(). Build variables are passed as bamboo.variable.<key>=<value> query parameters on the URL. A JSON body is silently ignored by Bamboo.
endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 228 `queueBuildWithStageSelection()` → POST /rest/api/latest/queue/{chainKey}?executeAllStages=... via postForm(); 2026-05-07-bamboo-write-ops-audit.md F-1 P0: 'queue endpoint requires bamboo.variable.<k>=<v> query params, NOT JSON body — JSON body silently ignored'; bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Architecture 'Build variables include dockerTagsAsJson'

verdict.reasoning: F-1 P0 from the write-ops audit: original implementation was passing variables as JSON body which Bamboo silently discards. Variables MUST be URL query parameters with the bamboo.variable.* prefix.

gotchas: CRITICAL P0: Variables MUST be passed as `bamboo.variable.<key>=<value>` QUERY parameters, NOT as a JSON request body. Bamboo silently ignores JSON bodies on this endpoint.
CRITICAL: The `X-Atlassian-Token: no-check` header is REQUIRED on all Bamboo write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE). Omitting it causes a 403 CSRF rejection.
The `executeAllStages=false&stage={name}` variant triggers up to and including the named manual stage — stage name must match exactly (case-sensitive).
On success, Bamboo returns HTTP 200 with the queued build info, NOT 201 or 204.
Non-admin PATs may lack permission to trigger certain plans — returns 403 with a plan-permission message.

endpoints.summary: Get the global build queue showing all currently queued builds across all plans.
endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/queue_global.json; bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/queue → OK; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-5 global-queue DEFERRED

verdict.reasoning: 51 builds queued at probe time — this endpoint surfaces org-wide build pressure, useful for the agent to diagnose slow builds. Deferred in the audit; could be adopted for a queue-depth diagnostic tool.

gotchas: Returns global queue across ALL plans, not filtered to the current plan.
The probe response only includes the size/count — expand may be needed to see per-build details.

endpoints.summary: Remove a queued build from the queue (cancel before it starts). Only valid while the build is in QUEUED state.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Result key of the queued build to cancel.

params.name: X-Atlassian-Token
params.description: CSRF bypass header, value `no-check`.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 357 `cancelBuild()` → DELETE /rest/api/latest/queue/{resultKey}; 2026-05-07-bamboo-write-ops-audit.md §write-inventory DELETE /rest/api/latest/queue/{resultKey}
gotchas: Only works on QUEUED builds — a build that has already started must be stopped via PUT /rest/api/latest/result/{key}/stop instead.
The X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header is required on DELETE as well as POST/PUT.
Returns 404 if the build key is no longer in the queue (already started or already cancelled).

endpoints.summary: Stop a currently-running build. Only valid for builds in BUILDING state.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Result key of the running build to stop.

params.name: X-Atlassian-Token
params.description: CSRF bypass header, value `no-check`.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 362 `stopBuild()` → PUT /rest/api/latest/result/{resultKey}/stop; 2026-05-07-bamboo-write-ops-audit.md §write-inventory PUT /rest/api/latest/result/{resultKey}/stop
gotchas: Only works on BUILDING builds — use DELETE /queue/{key} for queued builds instead.
The X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header is required.
May take several seconds for Bamboo agents to acknowledge the stop signal — the build may show BUILDING briefly after the PUT returns 200.

endpoints.summary: Rerun failed jobs in a completed build via a Struts admin action. Returns HTTP 302 on success. This is NOT a REST API endpoint — it is a Struts action under /build/admin/.

params.name: planKey
params.description: Plan key of the build to rerun.

params.name: buildNumber
params.description: Build number to rerun.

params.name: X-Atlassian-Token
params.description: CSRF bypass header, value `no-check`. Required for all Bamboo admin actions.

endpoints.requestBody: Form-encoded POST via postForm(). Parameters are passed as query parameters on the URL. No body required.
endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 326 `rerunFailedJobs()` → POST /build/admin/restartBuild.action?planKey={k}&buildNumber={n} via postForm(); 2026-05-07-bamboo-write-ops-audit.md F-6: 'restartBuild.action needs X-Atlassian-Token: no-check; returns 302 on success'
gotchas: CRITICAL: This is a Struts action under /build/admin/, NOT a REST API endpoint. It returns HTTP 302 on success, NOT 200. The HTTP client must be configured to NOT follow redirects, or treat 302 as success.
The X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header is required.
This endpoint is undocumented in the official Bamboo REST API docs — it is a legacy admin action that may change between Bamboo versions.
Only reruns failed jobs within the existing build — it does not create a new build result key.

endpoints.summary: Enable a disabled plan branch. A branch IS a plan, so this enables the branch plan key itself.

params.name: branchPlanKey
params.description: Branch plan key to enable (e.g. PROJ-PLAN-FEATURE1). Branch keys follow pattern ^.+-.+-\d+ but in practice are alphanumeric.

params.name: X-Atlassian-Token
params.description: CSRF bypass header, value `no-check`.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 347 `enablePlanBranch()` → POST /rest/api/latest/plan/{branchPlanKey}/enable via postForm(); bamboo/CLAUDE.md §Bamboo Server REST API 'BambooApiClient.enablePlanBranch → postForm (sets X-Atlassian-Token: no-check), surfaced as BambooService.enablePlanBranch(branchPlanKey): ToolResult<Unit>. ⚠ NOT probed against live DC 10.2.14 (added 2026-05-26); non-admin PATs likely get 403'

verdict.reasoning: Added 2026-05-26 but NOT probed against live DC 10.2.14. Non-admin PATs likely get 403 — surfaced as a plan-admin permission hint. Verify on a live server before relying on it.

gotchas: ⚠ NOT probed against live DC 10.2.14 — correctness unverified as of 2026-05-26.
Non-admin PATs likely receive HTTP 403 — map to a user-visible plan-admin permission hint.
A branch plan key follows the naming pattern of the master plan key plus a branch suffix — it is NOT the Git branch name.
The X-Atlassian-Token: no-check header is required.

endpoints.summary: List all linked repositories configured in Bamboo (global repository settings). Returns size and searchResults[].

params.name: max-results
params.description: Page size. Client uses 200.

params.name: start-index
params.description: Pagination cursor.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/repository_list.json (size:0, empty searchResults — admin-gated or org doesn't use Linked Repositories); BambooApiClient.kt line 485 `getLinkedRepositories()` → GET /rest/api/latest/repository?max-results=200&start-index={start}
gotchas: Returned empty (size: 0) in both probe bundles — this endpoint may be admin-gated, or the org does not use Bamboo's global Linked Repository feature.
An empty response does not indicate an error — simply no repositories are configured at the global level.
Paginated via start-index; uses the same paginate() loop as getPlans/getBranches.

endpoints.summary: List all plans that use a given linked repository.

params.name: repositoryId
params.description: Numeric ID of the linked repository.

endpoints.provenance: BambooApiClient.kt line 496 `getRepositoryUsedBy()` → GET /rest/api/latest/repository/{id}/usedBy
gotchas: The repository ID is a numeric Bamboo internal ID, not a repository name or slug.
If getLinkedRepositories() returns empty, this endpoint will never be called in practice.

endpoints.summary: List all build agents registered with Bamboo. Returns an array of {id, name, type, active, enabled, busy}.
endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/raw/agent_list.json (array of {id,name,type,active,enabled,busy}, 300+ agents); bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/agent → OK; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-4 agents DEFERRED

verdict.reasoning: 300+ agents visible in the probe org. Useful for a queue-pressure diagnostic — if all agents are busy, that explains slow build times. Deferred in the audit but low implementation cost.

gotchas: Returns all agents including disabled and offline ones — filter by `active: true` and `enabled: true` for available capacity.
`busy: true` means the agent is currently executing a build.
type ∈ {REMOTE, LOCAL, ELASTIC} — ELASTIC agents are Bamboo-managed cloud agents (rare on on-prem DC).

endpoints.summary: Global labels list. Returns 404 on DC 10.2.14 — this path does not exist on the probed version.

params.name: max-results
params.description: Result cap.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/labels → FAIL (404); 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-2 labels DEFERRED (404 failure reason)

verdict.reasoning: 404 on both probe bundles. Bamboo DC 10.2.14 does not expose a global /labels endpoint. Build-label access requires ?expand=labels.label on a specific result key.

gotchas: 404 on DC 10.2.14 — do not use.
To access labels for a specific build, use GET /rest/api/latest/result/{key}?expand=labels.label instead.

endpoints.summary: List Jira issues linked to a build result. Returns 404 on DC 10.2.14.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/result/{resultKey}/jiraIssues → FAIL (404)

verdict.reasoning: 404 on both probe bundles against DC 10.2.14. This endpoint may require a Bamboo-Jira application link with specific permissions, or may not be available in this DC version.

gotchas: 404 on DC 10.2.14 — do not use without verifying Bamboo-Jira application link configuration.
The Bamboo-Jira integration for issue tracking requires an Application Link configured in Bamboo admin settings.

endpoints.summary: List all deployment projects. Returns 200 with deployment project metadata.
endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/deploy/project/all → OK; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-6 deployment projects REJECTED

verdict.reasoning: Returned 200 on probe but explicitly rejected in the audit: the org's deployment workflow does not use Bamboo Deployments. The plugin's CI/CD surface uses the standard build pipeline only.

gotchas: Returns 200 on DC 10.2.14 but the org does not use Bamboo Deployments — data would be empty or misleading.
The /deploy/ path prefix is separate from /rest/api/latest/ in Bamboo's URL scheme.

endpoints.summary: List comments on a build result.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/result/{resultKey}/comment → OK; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-3 comments DEFERRED

verdict.reasoning: 200 on probe. Deferred in audit — low priority but could be useful for the agent to read human annotations on builds.

endpoints.summary: Get build labels via expand=labels.label. Alternative to the non-existent /labels global endpoint.

params.name: resultKey
params.description: Build result key.

params.name: expand
params.description: Must be `labels.label` to retrieve build labels.

endpoints.provenance: bundle-automation.unpacked/summary.md GET /rest/api/latest/result/{resultKey}?expand=labels.label → OK; 2026-05-07-bamboo-audit-recommendations.md §R-ADD-2 labels DEFERRED

verdict.reasoning: 200 on probe. The global /labels path 404s but per-result labels work. If build-tagging/labeling is ever needed, this is the correct path.

gotchas: The global GET /rest/api/latest/labels endpoint 404s — use this per-result path instead.
Deferred in the audit — the org does not actively use Bamboo build labels.
